expected_thesis.summary: GPT-5.6 and related frontier model releases are expected in June 2026 based on leaked internal testing signals.

expected_fit.minimum_expected_behavior: Recommend GPT-5.6 by June 30 as direct for the OpenAI portion of the source. Explain that it does not cover Sonnet 4.8 or Gemini 3.5 Pro.

must_mention: The GPT-5.6 market matches the named OpenAI model and June 2026 horizon.
The market does not resolve on Sonnet 4.8 or Gemini 3.5 Pro.
Leak-based source uncertainty should be disclosed.
must_not_claim: all three labs will definitely release in June

annotator_notes: Only the GPT-5.6 subclaim has a direct market in the provided candidate set.

expected_thesis.summary: Claude 4.8 Opus is expected to be released, but the exact post does not state a date.

expected_fit.minimum_expected_behavior: Treat Claude 5 release markets as indirect, not direct, because the source says Claude 4.8 Opus and gives no explicit deadline.

must_mention: The source names Claude 4.8 Opus, not Claude 5.
The exact post does not provide a clear date.
A Claude 5 by-date market is adjacent but not a clean expression.
must_not_claim: the post says end of May

annotator_notes: Corrects the inferred horizon in the Grok summary.

expected_thesis.summary: Diplomatic negotiations around Iran may be entering a final phase after a revised proposal focused on ending conflict and reopening the Strait of Hormuz.

expected_fit.minimum_expected_behavior: Use the Hormuz traffic market only as indirect because it measures a concrete reopening outcome, not acceptance of the revised proposal or diplomatic final-phase status.

must_mention: The source says negotiations may be entering final phase, which is uncertain.
Hormuz traffic normalization captures part of the reopening objective.
Traffic data does not resolve whether the revised proposal was accepted or a deal was reached.
must_not_claim: this is a direct expression of the proposal

annotator_notes: Grok labeled the traffic market direct; this pack downgrades to indirect because resolution is an outcome proxy.

expected_thesis.summary: Iran-US negotiations have three concrete unresolved disagreement points: nuclear talks, blocked funds, and Hormuz control.

expected_fit.minimum_expected_behavior: Classify permanent peace deal as indirect because it resolves a final binary outcome, not the three detailed negotiation conditions.

must_mention: The market resolves permanent peace, not partial resolution of nuclear, blocked-funds, and Hormuz-control conditions.
The source is granular and conditional.
Hormuz traffic is adjacent but misses the nuclear and blocked-funds components.
must_not_claim: the market resolves all three issues

annotator_notes: Good indirect case for over-specific source vs broad binary market.

expected_thesis.summary: Google's Gemini strategy is shifting from pure benchmark leadership toward native distribution and ubiquity across Android and Search.

expected_fit.minimum_expected_behavior: Say no clean expression exists. Do not recommend benchmark leaderboard markets as clean expressions of distribution strategy.

must_mention: The source explicitly contrasts benchmark leadership with native distribution.
No candidate market resolves distribution ubiquity across Android/Search.
Benchmark markets would be wrong because they test model quality, not product reach.
must_not_claim: a leaderboard market directly captures distribution strategy

expected_draft_contract.title_pattern: Will Google report Gemini integration across at least three billion active Android devices by Dec 31, 2026?

annotator_notes: Strong no-clean case for benchmark-proxy rejection.

expected_thesis.summary: AMD will ship Helios rack-scale AI systems with Venice and MI450X starting in H2 2026, while its AI accelerator position improves relative to NVIDIA.

expected_fit.minimum_expected_behavior: Say no clean expression exists unless a market directly resolves AMD Helios/MI450X shipment or AI accelerator share. Do not use stock-price gap as the thesis.

must_mention: The source mixes product shipment, revenue/share implications, and valuation-gap commentary.
No supplied market resolves AMD Helios/MI450X shipment timing.
A stock or valuation proxy would not cleanly express rack-scale shipment.
must_not_claim: AMD will definitely close the gap
stock price is a direct expression

expected_draft_contract.title_pattern: Will AMD publicly ship MI450X Helios rack-scale systems to at least one customer by Dec 31, 2026?

annotator_notes: No candidate markets supplied; securities-adjacent language requires caution.

expected_thesis.summary: AMD will remain with TSMC and avoid an Intel foundry deal in 2026 despite tight AI-related capacity.

expected_fit.minimum_expected_behavior: Say no clean expression exists. Do not convert broad capacity-tightness commentary into a market about AMD or TSMC unless the market directly resolves foundry partnership or allocation.

must_mention: The source is about AMD's foundry relationship and TSMC capacity tightness.
No candidate market resolves AMD avoiding an Intel foundry deal in 2026.
General AI chip supply markets would be wrong entity or wrong metric.
must_not_claim: TSMC capacity market directly resolves AMD foundry choice

expected_draft_contract.title_pattern: Will AMD announce an Intel foundry manufacturing deal by Dec 31, 2026?

expected_thesis.summary: China and the US are tightening control over cross-border frontier AI models, acquisitions, and partnerships.

expected_fit.minimum_expected_behavior: Say no clean expression exists unless a market directly resolves new AI export/acquisition restrictions. Avoid substituting chip-export markets if they do not cover model/acquisition controls.

must_mention: The source uses Meta-Manus as evidence for a broader policy trend.
No supplied market resolves frontier AI model export or acquisition restrictions.
Chip export-control markets would not necessarily capture model or acquisition controls.
must_not_claim: chip controls are direct expression of model export controls

expected_draft_contract.title_pattern: Will the US or China announce a new frontier AI model export or cross-border acquisition restriction by Dec 31, 2026?

expected_thesis.summary: Anthropic is allegedly paying SpaceX about $1.25B per month for compute access through 2029, implying frontier AI infrastructure needs exceed traditional data centers.

expected_fit.minimum_expected_behavior: Say no clean expression exists. Do not use aggregate AI capex or SpaceX IPO markets as clean expressions of a specific alleged Anthropic-SpaceX compute contract.

must_mention: The source depends on an alleged S-1 leak and should be treated cautiously.
No supplied market resolves the Anthropic-SpaceX compute contract.
Aggregate capex or IPO markets would miss the contract terms and counterparty.

expected_draft_contract.title_pattern: Will Anthropic or SpaceX confirm a compute infrastructure contract worth at least $10B annually by Dec 31, 2026?
resolution_logic_requirements: official confirmation or credible reporting consensus

annotator_notes: No clean market despite tempting AI capex narratives.

expected_thesis.summary: AI is capturing a very large share of VC funding while frontier labs and hyperscalers show high spend and limited measurable ROI.

expected_fit.minimum_expected_behavior: Treat Amazon 2026 capex as a weak proxy only. It captures one hyperscaler spend metric but not global VC share, lab burn rates, backlog concentration, or ROI.

must_mention: The source is aggregate and multi-entity.
Amazon capex is only one component of the broader AI funding and ROI thesis.
The market does not resolve VC share, Anthropic burn rate, or enterprise ROI.
must_not_claim: Amazon capex resolves the whole thesis

annotator_notes: Good weak-proxy case for wrong metric/entity scope.

expected_thesis.summary: SIBYL memory is moving toward public release and claims large reductions in hallucinations for agent memory over longer horizons.

expected_fit.minimum_expected_behavior: Say no clean expression exists. Do not use generic AI adoption markets as clean expressions of SIBYL public release or memory benchmark performance.

must_mention: The exact source claims 92% and 96% reductions, not an independently verified public benchmark.
No supplied market resolves SIBYL release or memory performance.
Enterprise agent adoption would be a different claim.

expected_draft_contract.title_pattern: Will SIBYL publicly release an agent memory product compatible with Claude Code or Codex by Dec 31, 2026?

annotator_notes: Corrects unsupported benchmark value from earlier Grok summary.

expected_thesis.summary: US homes are about 40% overpriced at current mortgage rates relative to pricing implied by much lower mortgage rates.

expected_fit.minimum_expected_behavior: Treat mortgage-rate threshold markets as weak proxies only. They resolve rate levels, not housing overpricing or home price correction.

must_mention: The source is a relative valuation claim about home prices and mortgage rates.
A mortgage-rate market resolves only rate thresholds.
It does not resolve the 40% overpricing estimate or home-price adjustment.

expected_thesis.summary: The Federal Reserve is not expected to cut rates until at least 2028 based on a forecast table and inflation concerns.

expected_fit.minimum_expected_behavior: Classify 2026 Fed cut markets as indirect because they test the near-term direction but not the full no-cuts-until-2028 horizon.

must_mention: The market is based on official FOMC decisions.
It covers 2026 rate cuts, not the full claim through 2027 and into 2028.
The risk-asset implication should not be treated as market advice.
must_not_claim: the market resolves no cuts until 2028

expected_thesis.summary: US fiscal policy conduct limits the effectiveness of interest-rate policy for containing COVID-era inflation.

expected_fit.minimum_expected_behavior: Say no clean expression exists. Do not substitute deficit or inflation markets as direct expressions of the academic causal thesis.

must_mention: The source is an academic causal thesis, not a single future event.
No supplied market resolves the effectiveness of rate policy conditional on fiscal policy.
Deficit or CPI markets would be adjacent at best.

expected_draft_contract.title_pattern: Will a peer-reviewed paper published by Dec 31, 2027 conclude that US fiscal policy materially limited Fed inflation control during 2021-2023?

expected_thesis.summary: Agentic AI is moving from demos and chatbots toward workflow integration, governance, memory, permissions, monitoring, optimization, and business outcomes.

expected_fit.minimum_expected_behavior: Say no clean expression exists. A good draft market must define measurable enterprise deployment or governance criteria.

must_mention: The source is a narrative vocabulary-shift thesis.
No supplied market resolves the shift from demos to workflow infrastructure.
A draft contract needs measurable adoption or deployment criteria.
must_not_claim: generic AI adoption market is direct

expected_draft_contract.title_pattern: Will at least 25 Fortune 500 companies publicly report production AI-agent workflow deployments by Dec 31, 2026?

expected_thesis.summary: Managers lack a clear standardized playbook for evaluating employee AI fluency in performance reviews.

expected_fit.minimum_expected_behavior: Say no clean expression exists. Avoid claiming generic AI adoption or productivity markets resolve HR evaluation standardization.

must_mention: The source is about lack of standardized AI fluency evaluation in performance reviews.
No supplied market resolves HR playbook adoption or manager evaluation standards.
A draft contract needs a measurable survey or public policy criterion.
must_not_claim: AI adoption market directly resolves HR evaluation standards

expected_draft_contract.title_pattern: Will more than 30% of Fortune 500 companies publicly include AI fluency criteria in performance reviews by Dec 31, 2027?
